title: Phase 5: Polish + production readiness (go-port)
description:
Epic tracking the remaining Phase 5 work from docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md.

Goal (from spec): production-grade deployment. Checkpoint: ready to replace the Python version in production.

Already done (commit bf5623f): admin user management endpoints + UI.

Remaining work tracked by this epic:
- nexorious-go-3g9: Migrate CLI surface to cobra subcommands
- nexorious-go-5bv: PostgreSQL-backed rate limiter
- nexorious-go-c9m: CleanupStaleJobsTask hourly scheduler job
- nexorious-go-8p7: Dockerfile (depends on cobra)
- nexorious-go-xus: Helm chart (depends on cobra + Dockerfile)
- nexorious-go-8x7: Verify and raise test coverage to >80%
- nexorious-go-chc: Documentation updates (depends on all of the above)

Dependency shape: cobra -> dockerfile -> helm -> docs, with rate-limiter / stale-jobs / coverage running in parallel and all feeding into docs last.
acceptance_criteria:
- All 7 child issues closed
- 'go test -cover ./...' >=80% on non-trivial packages
- Container image builds end-to-end
- Helm chart lints and deploys cleanly against a local cluster
- Production-readiness checkpoint from the spec satisfied
notes:
Implementation plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md
Maps each child issue to a Task section in the plan. Plan file is tracked in git (docs/superpowers/plans/ is checked in per CLAUDE.md).

title: Phase 5: Helm chart (adapted from nexorious chart)
description:
Port the existing Python nexorious Helm chart to nexorious-go. Adapt deployment templates to reference the new cobra subcommands (serve / migrate initContainer instead of --migrate-only flag).

Depends on:
- Cobra subcommand migration (chart templates reference the new CLI surface)
- Dockerfile (chart references a published image)

Source spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md
acceptance_criteria:
- charts/nexorious-go/ directory with Chart.yaml, values.yaml, templates/
- Deployment template with initContainer running 'migrate' subcommand
- Main container runs 'serve' subcommand
- ConfigMap/Secret wiring for DATABASE_URL, JWT secret, IGDB credentials
- Service + Ingress templates
- helm lint passes
- README documenting values
notes:
See Task 5 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md.
Step-by-step: adapt ../nexorious/deploy/helm/. Key deletions: NATS controller/service/PVC, internalApi*, worker/scheduler controllers (run in-process). Single 'nexorious' Deployment with migrate initContainer + serve main container. bjw-s common 4.6.2 dependency. helm lint + helm template smoke checks.
close_reason: Helm chart ports from Python nexorious chart; single nexorious controller with migrate initContainer + serve main; bjw-s common 4.6.2; non-root UID 10001 (matches Dockerfile); resources + readiness probe + nil-safe templates + URL-encoded DATABASE_URL; lint clean

title: Phase 5: Dockerfile (single-stage React+Go build)
description:
Add a Dockerfile that builds the React SPA (via 'make frontend'), then go-builds the binary, then assembles a minimal runtime image. Output should match the spec phrasing 'React build -> go build -> minimal runtime image'.

Depends on the cobra subcommand migration because the container ENTRYPOINT/CMD will reference 'serve' or 'migrate' subcommands rather than the legacy --migrate-only flag.

Source spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md
acceptance_criteria:
- Dockerfile at repo root
- Multi-stage: node stage builds ui/frontend/dist, go stage builds nexorious binary
- Final runtime image is minimal (distroless or alpine)
- Embeds React dist via existing //go:embed in ui/ui.go
- ENTRYPOINT uses cobra subcommands (post-migration)
- .dockerignore excludes ui/frontend/node_modules, .devenv, etc.
- Documented in DEV.md or README
notes:
See Task 4 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md.
Step-by-step: 3-stage Dockerfile (node 24-alpine builds ui/frontend/dist, golang 1.25-alpine builds binary with ldflags, debian:bookworm-slim runtime image with postgresql-client-18 for pg_dump/psql). ENTRYPOINT=/app/nexorious, CMD=serve. .dockerignore excludes node_modules/.devenv/.git/.beads/dist.
close_reason: Alpine 3.23 multi-stage Dockerfile + .dockerignore + DEV.md docs. Build verified outside sandbox: 47.6 MB image, version/--help/migrate subcommands functional, pg_dump 18.3 present

title: Phase 5: CleanupStaleJobsTask hourly scheduler job
description:
Add a scheduler job that detects stuck metadata_refresh jobs and marks them failed, preventing the dispatch duplicate-run guard from blocking future runs after a crash.

Detection criteria (from spec):
- job type = metadata_refresh
- status IN (pending, processing)
- no remaining unfinished job_items (all terminal, or none ever created)
- created_at older than threshold (default 4h, configurable)

Action: UPDATE jobs SET status='failed', error_message='stale_job_cleaned_up'

Runs hourly via gocron. Lives in internal/scheduler/ alongside existing scheduled tasks.

Source spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md
acceptance_criteria:
- CleanupStaleJobsTask function in internal/scheduler/
- Hourly registration in scheduler.go
- Threshold configurable via config (default 4h)
- Marks matching jobs failed with error_message='stale_job_cleaned_up'
- Integration test with testcontainers covering: stuck-pending, stuck-processing-no-items, stuck-processing-all-terminal-items, fresh-job-not-cleaned, completed-job-not-cleaned
notes:
See Task 2 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md.
Step-by-step: add STALE_JOB_THRESHOLD config (default 4h), implement scheduler.CleanupStaleJobs with a single UPDATE that uses NOT EXISTS on job_items, register hourly via gocron CronJob '0 * * * *'. Six test cases enumerated covering each cleanup-eligible / leave-alone permutation.
close_reason: CleanupStaleJobsTask registered hourly; 6 integration tests passing

title: Phase 5: Migrate CLI surface to cobra subcommands
description:
Replace the flag.BoolVar('--migrate-only') CLI surface in cmd/nexorious/main.go:53 with cobra subcommands: 'serve', 'migrate', 'migrate status', 'version'.

This is a prerequisite for the Dockerfile and Helm chart work because the container entrypoint and chart templates reference these subcommands. After the migration, update Helm chart, systemd units, and any tooling that uses --migrate-only.

Source spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md
acceptance_criteria:
- spf13/cobra added to go.mod
- 'serve' subcommand replaces default behaviour
- 'migrate' subcommand runs pending migrations and exits (replaces --migrate-only)
- 'migrate status' prints pending migrations without applying
- 'version' subcommand prints build version
- All existing --flag.* parsing preserved on 'serve' (or moved to viper-style config)
- Tooling using --migrate-only updated (search results from cmd/nexorious/main.go:53,134-175)
- Help text and tests
notes:
See Task 3 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md.
Step-by-step: add spf13/cobra dep, split cmd/nexorious/main.go into main.go (root cmd) + serve.go (current main body) + migrate.go (migrate + migrate status) + version.go. Add a Migrator.Status() helper if needed. --config becomes a persistent root flag. Bare ./nexorious still runs serve via RunE on root.
close_reason: Implemented cobra subcommands (serve/migrate/migrate status/version); spec + code reviews approved

title: Phase 5: PostgreSQL-backed rate limiter
description:
Add a multi-instance rate limiter implementation backed by PostgreSQL SELECT FOR UPDATE.

Phase 5 spec calls for the existing ratelimit.Limiter interface (already documented in CLAUDE.md as 'internal/ratelimit/ — interface + local (x/time/rate) and PostgreSQL implementations') but the directory does not exist in the tree at all. Both the interface and the local x/time/rate implementation must be created first, then the postgres.go implementation.

Source spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md
acceptance_criteria:
- internal/ratelimit/ package created with Limiter interface
- local.go using golang.org/x/time/rate (single-instance default)
- postgres.go using SELECT FOR UPDATE (opt-in via config)
- Config flag selects implementation
- Unit + integration tests (testcontainers for postgres impl)
- Wired into IGDB/Steam/PSN clients
notes:
See Task 1 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md.
Step-by-step: define Limiter interface + Config, NewLocal (x/time/rate), NewPostgres (SELECT FOR UPDATE), wire into IGDB client via constructor injection from main.go. The rate_limiter_tokens table already exists in initial migration so no migration work. Steam wiring deferred to a follow-up issue (Step 1.6).

title: Frontend MSW deletion-impact mock update
description:
## Task 5: Frontend test mock update

Update the MSW deletion-impact mock so `admin.test.ts` compiles and runs against the extended `UserDeletionImpact` interface.

### Files
- **Modify:** [ui/frontend/src/api/admin.test.ts](../../../ui/frontend/src/api/admin.test.ts) — around line 390, inside the `http.get(...deletion-impact)` handler

### Canonical reference
[Plan § Task 5](../plans/2026-05-13-admin-user-management.md).

### Change
Extend the returned mock object with `total_export_jobs`, `total_sync_jobs`, `total_sync_configs` (plausible non-zero sample values). Don't alter the existing count values — they may be referenced by test assertions. Don't change the `warning` text unless an assertion references it.

### Quality gates
From `ui/frontend/`:
```
npm run check
npm run test
```
Both must pass.
design: MSW mocks gate the existing Vitest suite. Without updating the mock the interface change from Task 3 leaves admin.test.ts in a TypeScript-broken state. This task closes that gap and runs in parallel with Task 4.
acceptance_criteria: ui/frontend/src/api/admin.test.ts MSW deletion-impact mock returns all 8 count fields (total_games, total_tags, total_import_jobs, total_export_jobs, total_sync_jobs, total_sync_configs, total_sessions) plus user_id, username, warning; existing assertion-referenced count values are preserved; warning string is unchanged unless test asserts on it; npm run check and npm run test pass from ui/frontend/
close_reason: Implemented and verified: MSW deletion-impact mock extended with 3 new fields; tests pass (23/23).

title: Frontend deletion-impact UI rows
description:
## Task 4: Frontend UI rows

Render the three new deletion-impact fields in the admin user detail page's delete confirmation table.

### Files
- **Modify:** [ui/frontend/src/routes/_authenticated/admin/users/$id.tsx](../../../ui/frontend/src/routes/_authenticated/admin/users/$id.tsx) — around line 576, where `deletionImpact.total_import_jobs` is rendered

### Canonical reference
[Plan § Task 4](../plans/2026-05-13-admin-user-management.md).

### Approach
1. **Read the existing total_import_jobs row first** to learn the exact JSX shape used (component names, classNames, label style). The plan shows illustrative `<TableRow>` JSX, but the actual file may use a different component.
2. Add three new rows immediately after it for `total_export_jobs` ("Export jobs"), `total_sync_jobs` ("Sync jobs"), `total_sync_configs` ("Sync configs"), copying the existing row's JSX shape verbatim.

### Quality gates
From `ui/frontend/`:
```
npm run check
npm run test
```
Both must pass.
design: The deletion confirmation table is the user-visible payoff for the spec's expanded counts. Matching the existing row JSX exactly (same component, same classNames) keeps the change visually consistent without design-system involvement.
acceptance_criteria: ui/frontend/src/routes/_authenticated/admin/users/$id.tsx renders three new rows for total_export_jobs, total_sync_jobs, total_sync_configs in the deletion-impact display, immediately after the existing total_import_jobs row; visual style (component, classNames) matches the existing rows verbatim; npm run check passes from ui/frontend/
close_reason: Implemented and verified: 3 new deletion-impact rows render correctly; npm run check and test pass cleanly.

title: Slumber admin user requests
description:
## Task 2: Slumber collection

Add the `admin_users` request folder to `slumber.yaml` — seven requests mirroring the new backend routes from Task 1.

### Files
- **Modify:** `slumber.yaml`

### Canonical reference
[Plan § Task 2](../plans/2026-05-13-admin-user-management.md) — provides the full YAML block to insert.

### Placement
Insert the new folder immediately after the existing `admin:` folder. Alphabetical: `admin_users` sorts after `admin`.

### Conventions (per CLAUDE.md § "Slumber Collection Maintenance")
- Every request uses `{{base_url}}` for the host (never `localhost:8000`)
- Every request uses `$ref: "#/.authenticated"` for bearer auth (token from the `login` response)
- For requests targeting a specific user, the URL uses jsonpath selection from `list_users` (same pattern as the existing `delete_backup` request)

### Seven requests required
`create_user` (POST), `list_users` (GET), `get_user` (GET), `update_user` (PUT), `reset_password` (PUT), `deletion_impact` (GET), `delete_user` (DELETE).

### Quality gate
```
slumber collection
```
Must exit zero with no parse errors and the new `admin_users` folder visible in the output.
design: Slumber is the manual API client used during development. Per CLAUDE.md § Slumber Collection Maintenance, every new route gets a request in the collection. Config-only task; gated on Task 1 so the requests can be exercised end-to-end against a running server.
acceptance_criteria: slumber.yaml contains an admin_users: request folder with 7 entries (create_user, list_users, get_user, update_user, reset_password, deletion_impact, delete_user); each entry uses {{base_url}} and $ref: "#/.authenticated"; slumber collection exits zero with no parse errors
close_reason: Implemented and verified: admin_users folder with 7 requests; slumber collection passes.

title: Frontend UserDeletionImpact type extension
design: The deletion-impact response shape on the Go port diverges from the Python version (adds three fields, drops one). The TypeScript interface must lead because Tasks 4 and 5 both consume it. Separating the type change from the UI and mock updates keeps blast radius small: if Task 4 or 5 need rework, the type change remains stable.
acceptance_criteria: ui/frontend/src/types/admin.ts UserDeletionImpact declares total_export_jobs, total_sync_jobs, total_sync_configs; field order matches backend JSON response (import → export → sync → sync_configs → sessions). Note: npm run check is expected to fail on dependent files (admin.test.ts, $id.tsx) until Tasks 4 and 5 land — that's not a regression for this task
close_reason: Implemented and verified: UserDeletionImpact extended with total_export_jobs, total_sync_jobs, total_sync_configs. Spec compliance and code quality reviews both APPROVED.

title: Backend admin user management handler + tests + router wiring
description:
## Task 1: Backend handler + tests + router wiring

Implement the seven admin user management endpoints (`POST/GET/PUT/DELETE /api/auth/admin/users[/:id[/password|/deletion-impact]]`), companion testcontainers tests covering every endpoint, and the router line that exposes the handler on the existing `adminGroup`.

### Files
- **Create:** `internal/api/admin_users.go`
- **Create:** `internal/api/admin_users_test.go`
- **Modify:** `internal/api/router.go` — five lines added after the existing `adminBackups` block (~line 283)

### Canonical reference
[Plan § Task 1](../plans/2026-05-13-admin-user-management.md) — full TDD step list (Steps 1.1 through 1.13). Every endpoint behaviour, DTO shape, error message and status code is specified in [the spec](../specs/2026-05-13-admin-user-management-design.md).

### Approach
TDD per endpoint. Steps 1.1–1.13 in the plan walk through: scaffold file → write failing test → implement → green. The plan provides the test scaffolding helpers (`getAuth`, `putJSONAuth`, `deleteAuth`, `loginAs`) verbatim — copy them to the top of `admin_users_test.go`.

### Critical seams

Handler pattern mirrors [internal/api/sync.go](../../../internal/api/sync.go):
```go
type AdminUsersHandler struct{ db *bun.DB }
func NewAdminUsersHandler(db *bun.DB) *AdminUsersHandler
func (h *AdminUsersHandler) RegisterRoutes(g *echo.Group)
```

**Route ordering:** register `/:id/password` and `/:id/deletion-impact` before `/:id` — Echo v5 does not auto-sort.

**Reused code:**
- `bcryptCost = 12` constant in [internal/api/auth.go](../../../internal/api/auth.go)
- `auth.HashToken` in [internal/auth/jwt.go](../../../internal/auth/jwt.go)
- Test helpers (`setupAuthTestDB`, `insertAuthTestUser`, `insertAuthTestSession`, `newTestEcho`, `testCfg`, `postJSON`, `postJSONAuth`) at the top of [internal/api/auth_test.go](../../../internal/api/auth_test.go)

**Cascade delete:** every `user_id` FK has `ON DELETE CASCADE` (seven tables — verified). A single `DELETE FROM users WHERE id = ?` removes everything.

**Session invalidation:**
- Password reset → `DELETE FROM user_sessions WHERE user_id = ?` (refresh tokens otherwise survive)
- `is_active = false` → same wipe (belt-and-suspenders; JWTMiddleware also rejects on next request)
- `is_admin` toggle → **no invalidation**; JWTMiddleware re-reads from DB per request

### Router wiring
Inside the `if db != nil { ... }` block in [internal/api/router.go](../../../internal/api/router.go), after the existing `adminBackups` registrations and before `// Sync routes`:
```go
        // Admin user management routes (JWT + admin required)
        auh := NewAdminUsersHandler(db)
        auh.RegisterRoutes(adminGroup)
```

### Quality gates
Must all pass before commit:
```
go test -timeout 300s ./...
golangci-lint run
```
design:
Core backend deliverable bundled into one task because all 7 endpoints share DTOs, validation, the handler struct, and the testcontainers harness — per-endpoint splits would cause divergent error formats and inconsistent self-protection logic. TDD per endpoint keeps the unit small.

Key decisions:
- Handler pattern mirrors SyncHandler (handler struct + NewX + RegisterRoutes).
- Routes attach to existing adminGroup (JWT + admin middleware already wired in router.go).
- DB cascades handle related-row cleanup on user delete (single DELETE FROM users; no manual cascade like Python).
- Session invalidation only on password reset and is_active=false. JWTMiddleware re-reads is_active/is_admin per request, so role changes need no extra invalidation.
- bcryptCost = 12 reused from internal/api/auth.go.
- Static /:id/password and /:id/deletion-impact routes registered before parameterised /:id (Echo v5 doesn't auto-sort).
acceptance_criteria: internal/api/admin_users.go exists with AdminUsersHandler + NewAdminUsersHandler + RegisterRoutes + all 7 handler methods; internal/api/admin_users_test.go covers happy path + 401/403/404/400 + self-protection paths for every endpoint and includes cascade delete + session invalidation integration tests; internal/api/router.go wires NewAdminUsersHandler(db).RegisterRoutes(adminGroup) after the existing adminBackups registrations; go test -timeout 300s ./... passes; golangci-lint run passes
close_reason: Implemented and verified: 7 admin user endpoints + 39 tests (incl. cascade and session invalidation) + router wiring. go test and golangci-lint clean. Spec compliance and code quality reviews both APPROVED.

title: Phase 5 — Admin User Management
description:
## Phase 5 — Admin User Management

Implement the seven `/api/auth/admin/users/*` endpoints called by the frontend, plus the frontend updates required by the extended deletion-impact response (adds `total_export_jobs`, `total_sync_jobs`, `total_sync_configs`; drops `total_wishlist_items`).

### Canonical references
- **Spec:** [docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-admin-user-management-design.md](../specs/2026-05-13-admin-user-management-design.md)
- **Plan:** [docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-admin-user-management.md](../plans/2026-05-13-admin-user-management.md)
- **Branch:** `phase-5-admin-user-management`

### Task structure
- **Task 1** — Backend handler + tests + router wiring. No dependencies.
- **Task 2** — Slumber collection. Depends on Task 1.
- **Task 3** — Frontend type extension. No dependencies.
- **Task 4** — Frontend UI rows. Depends on Task 3.
- **Task 5** — Frontend test mock. Depends on Task 3.

Backend chain (1 → 2) and frontend chain (3 → 4, 3 → 5) can run in parallel.

### Phase context
This is the first item in [Phase 5 — Polish + Production Readiness](../specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md). The other phase 5 items (PG rate limiter, cobra CLI refactor, stale-job cleanup, Dockerfile, Helm chart, test coverage, docs) are separate epics.
close_reason: All 5 tasks completed: backend handler+tests+router (Task 1), slumber collection (Task 2), frontend type+UI+mock (Tasks 3-5). All quality gates passed.

title: Phase 5: Documentation updates
description:
Phase 5 spec item: 'Documentation updates' before replacing the Python version in production.

Concrete update targets, to be confirmed during execution:
- README.md (production deployment, container usage)
- DEV.md (cobra CLI surface, coverage commands)
- CLAUDE.md (refresh Quick Reference commands and any architectural changes from phase 5)
- Helm chart README
- Migration guide from Python nexorious to nexorious-go (optional)

Depends on the other phase 5 features so docs can describe their final shape.

Source spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md
acceptance_criteria:
- README updated for production deployment
- DEV.md reflects current dev workflow (cobra subcommands)
- CLAUDE.md Quick Reference matches reality
- Helm values documented
- Production-readiness checkpoint from the spec satisfied
notes:
See Task 7 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md.
Targets: CLAUDE.md Quick Reference (cobra subcommands, coverage command), DEV.md (CLI surface + test coverage sections), README.md Deployment section (docker build + Helm install snippets). charts/nexorious-go/README.md is created as part of Task 5.

title: Phase 5: Verify and raise test coverage to >80%
description:
Phase 5 spec target: '>80% coverage with testcontainers-go'. Current coverage is unverified.

Steps:
1. Run 'go test -cover ./...' and capture per-package coverage
2. Identify packages below 80%
3. Add tests (testcontainers-go where DB integration is needed) until target is met
4. Document running coverage in DEV.md

Source spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-03-go-port-design-phase-5.md
acceptance_criteria:
- 'go test -cover ./...' run and snapshot recorded in the issue notes
- All non-trivial packages at >=80%
- Any deliberate exclusions (e.g. main, generated code) documented
notes:
See Task 6 in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-phase-5-production-readiness.md.

Baseline coverage (2026-05-13, before plan execution):
- internal/api: 67.4%
- internal/auth: 89.0%
- internal/backup: 55.6%
- internal/config: 100.0%
- internal/middleware: 100.0%
- internal/migrate: 62.2%
- internal/scheduler: 8.6%
- internal/services/igdb: 48.5%
- internal/services/matching: 100.0%
- internal/services/platformresolution: 100.0%
- internal/worker: 85.7%
- internal/worker/tasks: 46.9%

Below 80% (need work): api, backup, migrate, scheduler, services/igdb, worker/tasks.
Note: scheduler's 8.6% is because most of its functions are NOT exercised by current tests — adding CleanupStaleJobs tests (Task 2) will move this. The Task 1 ratelimit tests will lift the package's coverage as well.

title: Rename Migrator.DetermineStateForTest to drop ForTest suffix
description: Follow-up from nexorious-go-3g9 code review. internal/migrate/migrator.go exposes DetermineStateForTest which is used by both production CLI code (cmd/nexorious/serve.go:107, cmd/nexorious/migrate.go:107,144) and tests. The ForTest suffix is misleading. Rename to DetermineState() (or similar) and keep a thin ForTest wrapper only if needed.

title: Add signal.NotifyContext to migrate/migrate-status subcommands
description: Follow-up from nexorious-go-3g9 code review. Neither cmd/nexorious/migrate.go runMigrate nor runMigrateStatus uses signal.NotifyContext, so Ctrl-C during a long migration won't get graceful ctx.Done propagation. Important for k8s pre-stop hooks. Parity with legacy --migrate-only, but worth fixing.

title: DRY: route runServe through loadEnvAndConfig
description: Follow-up from nexorious-go-3g9 code review. cmd/nexorious/serve.go:47-65 duplicates the env-loading logic that cmd/nexorious/migrate.go:53-67 factored into loadEnvAndConfig. Route runServe through the shared helper to eliminate drift risk.
